versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 22 This is what the LORD says:
from the land of the north;
a great nation is stirred up
from the ends of the earth.
23 They grasp the bow and spear;
Their voice roars like the sea,
lined up like men in formation
against you, O Daughter of Zion.”
pain like that of a woman in labor.
25 Do not go out to the fields;
For the enemy has a sword;
dress yourselves in sackcloth and roll in ashes.
as you would for an only son,
27 “I have appointed you to examine My people like ore,
so you may know and try their ways.
blasting away the lead with fire.
for the wicked are not purged.
because the LORD has rejected them.”

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 6**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **An Invasion from the North** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 6** remains the first control on meaning.
